rationale: In the body text, Monga and Houston (2006) is cited at a specific procedural point: the authors state that their two questions assessing concurrent-task mental effort are based on that work. This is a concrete measurement-borrowing role rather than a generic literature-list mention. The cited paper is not used to build the main serial-position theory, but it still materially informs how a key manipulation check is implemented.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Monga and Houston (2006) as the source for mental-effort manipulation-check items in Study 1b. It applies that prior measurement approach in a new experimental context on sampled experiential products.
